title: c-VEP dataset from Thielen et al. (2021)
description: A code-modulated visual evoked potential (cVEP) brain-computer interface dataset from 30 healthy participants performing offline and online spelling tasks. This derivative dataset demonstrates a calibration-free BCI approach using an encoding model that systematically reduces training data requirements, ultimately achieving high communication rates without any user-specific calibration data. The study validates neural encoding models as an alternative to traditional event-related potential templates for practical plug-and-play BCI applications.
methods_description: EEG recorded at 512 Hz from 8 electrodes (Fpz, Iz, O1, O2, Oz, POz, T7, T8) using Biosemi ActiveTwo hardware with sintered Ag/AgCl active electrodes. Stimuli were luminance-modulated pseudo-random Gold codes (126-bit sequences) presented at 60 Hz on an iPad Pro. Offline experiment: 30 participants completed 5 blocks of 20 trials each using a 4×5 calculator grid. Online experiment: 11 participants used a keyboard layout with dynamic stopping rule. Data preprocessed with 2-30 Hz Butterworth filtering and downsampled to 120 Hz. Classification employed template-matching with reconvolution encoding model.
